title: Compiler crashes when a constant function calls undefined function
body:
Hello,

I found that the compiler segfaults when a constant function with `return` tries to call an undefined function:
```systemverilog
module test1;
   function [31:0] func1 (input [31:0] x);
      return undef_func(x);
   endfunction : func1

   localparam X = func1(1);
endmodule : test1
```
```
$ iverilog -g2009 -Wall test1.sv
test1.sv:3: error: No function named `undef_func' found in this context (test1.func1).
Segmentation fault
```

If the third line above was `func1 = undef_func(x);` (i.e. "classical" Verilog style) no segfaults occur.
code_blocks:
$ iverilog -g2009 -Wall test1.sv
test1.sv:3: error: No function named `undef_func' found in this context (test1.func1).
Segmentation fault
